game_name: Desert Strike: Return to the Gulf

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this helicopter action game

special_mechanics.name: Helicopter Control
special_mechanics.description: Player controls an AH-64 Apache helicopter with momentum-based camera system designed to mimic realistic helicopter movements. Vehicle movement is non-linear and strategic rather than frantic.

physics_values.notes: Camera system has momentum; no specific acceleration/velocity values documented

special_mechanics.name: Fuel System
special_mechanics.description: Helicopter has finite fuel that depletes over time. Fuel must be replenished via fuel barrels. Running out of fuel causes crash and loss of life.

special_mechanics.name: Armor/Damage System
special_mechanics.description: Helicopter has limited armor depleted by enemy fire. Armor can be replenished via power-ups or by airlifting rescued friendlies/captives to landing zones.

special_mechanics.name: Ammunition Management
special_mechanics.description: Limited ammunition for machine gun, Hydra rockets, and Hellfire missiles. Must be replenished via ammo crates. Weapon choice affects carrying capacity.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Game is helicopter action/shoot-em-up, not a platformer. Movement physics are vehicle-based with camera momentum system rather than character platforming mechanics. No specific numeric physics parameters documented in available sources. Game design emphasizes strategic planning (fuel/ammo management) over twitch mechanics.
sources_used: tcrf.net - Desert Strike SNES debug info
wikipedia.org - Desert Strike article with gameplay mechanics
